item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents consistently reject the statement from first to final turn. Agent A frames Indonesian public-interest language as vulnerable to abuse against weak groups, laborers, minorities, and critics. Agent B repeatedly uses a US civil-liberties frame, treating individual rights as a guardrail against majority or state power. Both acknowledge that shared/public interests matter, but only under clear rights-based limits; this is not a visible position shift.
